CREATE TABLE employees (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), department_id INT);
CREATE TABLE departments (id INT PRIMARY KEY, department_name VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department_id) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 1), (2, 'Bob', 2), (3, 'Charlie', 3), (4, 'David', 1), (5, 'Eve', 2);
SELECT e.name, d.department_name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id;
SELECT e.name FROM employees e WHERE e.id IN (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE department_id = 1);
SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(e.id) AS employee_count FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.department_name;
SELECT e.name FROM employees e WHERE e.name LIKE '%a%';
SELECT AVG(salary) FROM (SELECT salary FROM employees WHERE department_id = (SELECT id FROM departments WHERE department_name = 'Finance')) AS finance_salaries;
SELECT e.name FROM employees e WHERE e.department_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM departments WHERE department_name = 'HR');
CREATE VIEW employee_department AS SELECT e.name, d.department_name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id;
UPDATE employees SET department_id = 2 WHERE name = 'Charlie';
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department_id IN (SELECT id FROM departments WHERE department_name IN ('HR', 'Finance'));
SELECT d.department_name, COALESCE(SUM(salary), 0) FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.department_name;
